questionText: The Colour coding on a carbon resistor is shown in the given figure. The resistance value of the given resistor is :
subject: Physics

questionText:
A 200 $$\Omega $$  resistor has a certain color code. If one
replaces the red color by green in the code, the
new resistance will be :
subject: Physics
solution:
200 $$\Omega $$ = Red + Black + Brown

Green = 5

So, Green + Black + Brown = 500 $$\Omega $$

questionText: A carbon resistance has a following colour code. What is the value of the resistance ?
subject: Physics
solution:
From colour coding table,

Green line represents number = 5

Orange line represents number = 3

Yellow line represent multiplier = 104

Golden line represents tollerence = $$ \pm $$ 5%

$$ \therefore $$   Resistance = 53 $$ \times $$ 104 $$ \pm $$ 5%

= 530 k$$\Omega $$ $$ \pm $$ 5%

questionText: A resistance is shown in the figure. Its value and tolerance are given respectively by :
subject: Physics
solution:
From color code table :

For Red value is 2

For Violet value is 7

For Orange multiplier is 103

For Silver tolarence is 10%

$$ \therefore $$ Resistance and tolerance is

= 27 $$ \times $$ 103 $$ \pm $$ 10%

= 27 k$$\Omega $$ $$ \pm $$ 10%
